# Dani Gordon's Landing Page

*A personal professional landing page built to introduce Dani L. Gordon to recruiters and hiring managers.*

A static personal landing page for Dani L. Gordon, a Business Analytics & Information Systems student at the University of Iowa graduating in May 2026. The page provides a centralized, professional web presence that goes beyond a resume — communicating her skills, work history, technical projects, and career goals in one clean, accessible space. It is designed for recruiters and hiring managers at mid-size to large companies in Chicago looking for candidates in business analysis, marketing analytics, operations, or consulting roles.

---

## Features

*Update this list if you add or remove features.*

- **Sticky navigation bar** with anchor links to every section for fast browsing
- **Hero section** with circular professional headshot, name, and tagline
- **Three-category skills section** with color-coded pill badges for Technical Tools, Analytical Skills, and Business & Professional Skills
- **Work experience cards** listing all roles with employer, title, dates, and bullet-point accomplishments
- **Three-column project grid** showcasing data analysis projects with tech stack labels
- **Education section** including GPA, Dean's List honors, and leadership involvement
- **Contact section** with labeled icon badges linking to LinkedIn, email, and GitHub
- **Fully responsive** layout — works on all screen sizes from 320px wide to desktop

---

## Tech Stack

*Update this table if you add new libraries, tools, or frameworks.*

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Semantic page structure and content |
| CSS3 | All styling, layout, and responsiveness — no inline styles |
| Google Fonts — Playfair Display | Heading typography |
| Google Fonts — Inter | Body typography |
| CSS Custom Properties | Centralized design tokens (colors, spacing, fonts) |
| CSS Grid & Flexbox | Responsive layout for project cards and skill tags |
| Azure Static Web Apps | Automated CI/CD deployment from the `main` branch |
| GitHub Actions | Workflow that builds and deploys on every push to `main` |

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

*List anything extra here if your setup changes — e.g., a Node.js version or build tool.*

This is a plain static site with no build step and no dependencies to install. You only need:

- A modern web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari)
- A code editor such as [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) (optional, for editing)
- [Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads) (optional, for cloning)

### Installation

*Replace the repository URL below with your own if you fork this project.*

1. Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/DaniLynGordon/Dgordon2-LandingPage.git
```

2. Navigate into the project folder:

```bash
cd Dgordon2-LandingPage
```

3. Open the site in your browser:

```bash
# Option A — simply open the file directly
open index.html

# Option B — serve it locally with Python (recommended to avoid any path issues)
python3 -m http.server 5000
```

4. If using Python, visit `http://localhost:5000` in your browser.

No `npm install`, no build command, and no environment variables are required.

---

## Usage

*Update this section if you add any configuration options, forms, or dynamic features.*

Once the page is open in a browser, use the sticky top navigation bar to jump between sections: About, Education, Skills, Experience, Projects, and Contact.

- Click **LinkedIn**, **Email**, or **GitHub** in the Contact section to reach out directly — all three open in a new tab.
- The page is read-only and fully static; there are no forms, logins, or data inputs.
- To personalize this template for your own use, edit the content in `index.html` and update the color palette CSS custom properties at the top of `css/stylesheet.css`.

---

## Project Structure

```
Dgordon2-LandingPage/
├── index.html              # Main page — all HTML content and section structure
├── css/
│   └── stylesheet.css      # All styles: layout, typography, colors, responsive breakpoints
├── Images/
│   └── Dani_Gordon.png     # Professional headshot used in the hero section
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── azure-static-web-apps-calm-beach-0842aef0f.yml  # Azure CI/CD deployment workflow
├── PRD.md                  # Product Requirements Document — goals, audience, required content
├── STANDARDS.md            # Technical and design standards for this project
└── README.md               # This file
```

---

## Changelog

*Add a new entry here each time you release an update.*

### v1.0.0 — March 31, 2026

Initial release of the personal landing page.

**Included in this release:**
- Sticky navigation bar with anchor links to all six sections
- Hero section with circular headshot, name, tagline, and location
- About Me bio section
- Education section with University of Iowa, GPA 3.9/4.0, Dean's List, and Leadership & Involvement
- Three-group Skills section: Technical Tools, Analytical Skills, and Business & Professional Skills
- Work Experience section covering Archway Pet (Marketing Lead + Business Operations Intern), Mauro Provisions, and Hudson Burnham
- Three-column Projects grid: Data4Good Nonprofit Analytics, Relational Database Design & SQL Analytics, NBA Performance & Attendance Data Analysis
- Contact section with LinkedIn, email, and GitHub icon badges
- Fully responsive design (320px through desktop)
- WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility compliance
- Automated deployment to Azure Static Web Apps via GitHub Actions

---

## Known Issues / To-Do

*Check these off as you complete them, and add new items as you discover them.*

- [ ] No dark mode support — the site currently only uses a light blush/white palette
- [ ] Contact form not implemented — visitors can only reach out by clicking the pre-set badge links
- [ ] No analytics or visitor tracking — success metrics from the PRD (visits, outreach, interviews) cannot currently be measured without adding a tool such as Google Analytics
- [ ] The headshot file path uses a capital `I` in `Images/` — standardizing to lowercase `images/` would follow web convention and improve cross-platform compatibility

---

## Roadmap

*These are aspirational — update or remove items as the project evolves.*

- **Dark mode toggle** — Add a CSS-based dark mode using `prefers-color-scheme` and an optional toggle button
- **Contact form** — Integrate a lightweight form service (e.g., Formspree) so visitors can message directly from the page
- **Analytics integration** — Add Google Analytics or Plausible to track visits and measure the success metrics defined in the PRD
- **Downloadable resume button** — Add an optional button linking to a hosted PDF version of the resume (currently excluded per STANDARDS.md)
- **Project detail pages** — Expand each project card into its own page with screenshots, methodology, and key findings

---

## Contributing

*Update the repository URL below if this project is forked.*

This project is a personal portfolio site, but suggestions and improvements are welcome. To contribute:

1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Create a new branch for your changes:

```bash
git checkout -b feature/your-improvement-name
```

3. Make your edits in `index.html` and/or `css/stylesheet.css`
4. Commit your changes with a descriptive message:

```bash
git commit -m "Add dark mode toggle"
```

5. Push the branch to your fork:

```bash
git push origin feature/your-improvement-name
```

6. Open a pull request against the `main` branch and describe what you changed and why.

## Acknowledgements

- **Google Fonts** — [Playfair Display](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Playfair+Display) and [Inter](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Inter) used for typography
- **Shields.io** — Badge generation for the README header
- **Azure Static Web Apps** — Free hosting and CI/CD for static sites
- **Claude (Anthropic)** — Initial HTML structure, CSS stylesheet, and content organization were generated with Claude based on the author's PRD, STANDARDS.md, and resume, then reviewed and approved by the author
